briefdescription.para: Triggers decryption operation for black key.
Disables IV and Key save features for KUP.
Enables IV and Key save features for KUP.
Enables authentication of data pushed in AES engine.
Disables authentication of data pushed in AES engine.
This function waits for AES completion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance

briefdescription.para: This function configures and loads AES key from selected key source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Variable is of type XSecure_AesKeySrc which mentions the key source to be loaded into AES engine
Size of the key selected

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE - If a timeout has occurred

briefdescription.para: This function waits for AES key decryption completion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc KeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize, u64 IvAddr, u32 Mode)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesOpInit

briefdescription.para: This function validates and updates the mode of the AES and loads key and transfers IV to the AES engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Key Source for decryption of the data
Size of the AES key to be used for decryption: XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size
Address to the buffer holding IV
AES operation mode (XSECURE_AES_MODE_ENC for encryption or XSECURE_AES_MODE_DEC for decryption)

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XST_FAILURE - On failure to configure switch

briefdescription.para: This function configures the PMC DMA channels.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance.
DMA SRC and DEST channel configuration
Size of data in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u8 IsLastChunk)
memberdef.name: XSecureAesUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the AES engine with provided input and output addresses.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the input buffer.
Address of output buffer.
Size of data to be updated by AES engine.
If this is the last update of data to be processed, this parameter should be set to TRUE otherwise FALSE

briefdescription.para: This function transfers IV to AES engine.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address to the buffer holding IV

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_INVALID_PARAM - If Iv address is zero

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc KeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize, u64 IvAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesKeyLoadandIvXfer

briefdescription.para: This function initializes AES engine for encryption or decryption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Key Source for decryption of the data
Size of the AES key to be used for decryption: XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size
Address to the buffer holding IV

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u8 IsLastChunk)
memberdef.name: XSecure_ValidateAndUpdateData

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the data to AES engine and stores the resultant data at specified address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the encrypted data which needs to be encrypted
Address of output buffer where the encrypted data to be updated
Size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas number of bytes shall be aligned as follows: 16 byte aligned when it is not the last chunk or 4 byte aligned when the data is the last chunk
If this is the last update of data to be encrypted, this parameter should be set to TRUE otherwise FALSE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful encryption of the data
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Validate the size for last chunk
Wait for AES Done for last chunk in ECB mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used upon Last encrypt update.

detaileddescription.para: AES engine also sends GCM tag along with encrypted data,copy the GCM tag to local buffer to properly flush the DMA to avoid data corruption,if the same DMA is used for different operation before AES encrypt final.This is also used to store the generated GCM Tag during AES update AAD in GMAC mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
DMA SRC and DEST channel configuration

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful copy of the GCM tag
Invalidate Cache before and after dma transfer to ensure cache coherency for a72 and r5 and RISCV processors
Zeroize the keys after AES operation completion

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the AES instance pointer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Pointer to the XPmcDma instance

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_DMA_COMPONENT_IS_NOT_READY - PMC DMA component is not ready

simplesect.para: All the inputs are accepted in little endian format, but AES engine accepts the data in big endianness, this will be taken care while passing data to AES engine

briefdescription.para: This function enables or disables DPA counter measures in AES engine based on user input.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
User choice to enable/disable DPA CM. Set to TRUE to enable AES DPA counter measure (Default setting) or FALSE to disable AES DPA counter measure

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XSECURE_AES_DPA_CM_NOT_SUPPORTED - If DPA CM is disabled on chip (Enabling/Disabling in AES engine does not impact functionality)
Set DPA counter measures as per the user input

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc KeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize, u64 KeyAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesWriteKey

briefdescription.para: This function writes the key provided into the specified AES key registers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance

Key Source to be selected to which provided key should be updated: XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_0, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_1, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_2, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_3, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_4, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_5, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_6, XSECURE_AES_USER_KEY_7, or XSECURE_AES_BH_KEY
A variable of type XSecure_AesKeySize, which holds the size of the input key to be written: XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size
Address of a buffer which should contain the key to be written

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful key written on AES registers
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Write key into the specified AES key registers

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the AES engine with Additional Authenticated Data(AAD).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the additional authenticated data
Size of additional authenticated data in bytes, whereas number of bytes provided should be quad-word aligned(multiples of 16 bytes) for Versal For Versal Net, byte aligned data is accepted

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful update of AAD
XST_FAILURE - On failure to update AAD

simplesect.para: The API must be called after XSecure_AesEncryptInit() or XSecure_AesDecryptInit()

listitem.para: Enable AAD by writing to the register
Configure DMA and transfer AAD to AES engine

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc DecKeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySrc DstKeySrc, u64 IvAddr, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesKekDecrypt

briefdescription.para: This function decrypts the KEK/obfuscated key which is found encrypted in boot header/eFUSE/BBRAM.

detaileddescription.para: It then places it in the specified red key register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Select key source which holds KEK and needs to be decrypted
Select the key in which decrypted red key should be updated
Address of IV holding buffer for decryption of the key
A variable of type XSecure_AesKeySize, which specifies the size of the key: XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size

listitem.para: Load AES key to registers from PUF key
Configure the AES engine for Key decryption and starts operation

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc KeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize, u64 IvAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesDecryptInit

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the AES engine for decryption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Key Source for decryption of the data
Size of the AES key to be used for decryption (XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size)
Address to the buffer holding IV

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XSECURE_AES_ZERO_PUF_KEY_NOT_ALLOWED - If keysrc is puf and puf key is zero

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u8 IsLastChunk)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesDecryptUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the AES engine for decryption with provided data and stores the decrypted data at specified address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the encrypted data which needs to be decrypted
Address of output buffer where the decrypted to be updated

Size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas number of bytes shall be aligned as follows for Versal: 16 byte aligned when it is not the last chunk or 4 byte aligned when the data is the last chunk. For Versal Net, byte aligned data is accepted
If this is the last update of data to be decrypted, this parameter should be set to TRUE otherwise FALSE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful decryption of the data
XSECURE_AES_UNALIGNED_SIZE_ERROR - If input IsLastChunk is invalid
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the GCM tag provided for the data decrypted till the point.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of a buffer which should holds GCM Tag

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful GCM tag verification
XSECURE_AES_GCM_TAG_MISMATCH User provided GCM tag does not match calculated tag
Update AES engine with the GCM Tag data to verify the GCM Tag
Verify whether GCM Tag is matched or not

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u64 GcmTagAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesDecryptData

briefdescription.para: This function decrypts the size (length) number of bytes of the passed in InDataAddr (source) buffer and stores the decrypted data in the OutDataAddr (destination) buffer and verifies GcmTagAddr.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the encrypted data which needs to be decrypted
Address of output buffer where the decrypted to be updated
Size of data to be decrypted in bytes, whereas number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4 for Versal For Versal Net, byte aligned data is acceptable
Address of a buffer which should contain GCM Tag

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful decryption and GCM tag verification
XSECURE_AES_UNALIGNED_SIZE_ERROR - Size is not aligned for non-last chunk
Update AES engine with encrypted data and get the decrypted data output

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, XSecure_AesKeySrc KeySrc, XSecure_AesKeySize KeySize, u64 IvAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesEncryptInit

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the AES engine for encryption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Key Source for encryption
Size of the AES key to be used for encryption (XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_128 for 128 bit key size or XSECURE_AES_KEY_SIZE_256 for 256 bit key size)
Address to the buffer holding IV

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
XSECURE_AES_ZERO_PUF_KEY_NOT_ALLOWED - If keysrc is puf and puf key is zero

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u8 IsLastChunk)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesEncryptUpdate

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the AES engine for encryption with provided data and stores the encrypted data at specified address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the data which needs to be encrypted
Address of output buffer where the encrypted data to be updated
Size of data to be encrypted in bytes. For Versal, data shall be 16 byte aligned for non-last chunks and 4 byte aligned for the last chunk. For Versal Net, byte aligned data is acceptable
If this is the last update of data to be encrypted, this parameter should be set to TRUE otherwise FALSE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful encryption of the data
XSECURE_AES_UNALIGNED_SIZE_ERROR - If input IsLastChunk is invalid
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function updates the GCM tag for the encrypted data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address to the buffer of GCM tag size, where the API updates GCM tag

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On successful GCM tag updation
XSECURE_AES_INVALID_MODE - If input mode is invalid
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Copying from local buffer which is already stored during encrypt update.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 InDataAddr, u64 OutDataAddr, u32 Size, u64 GcmTagAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesEncryptData

briefdescription.para: This function encrypts size (length) number of bytes of the passed in InDataAddr (source) buffer and stores the encrypted data along with its associated 16 byte tag in the OutDataAddr (destination) buffer and GcmTagAddr (buffer) respectively.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the data which needs to be encrypted
Address of output buffer where the encrypted data to be updated
Size of data to be encrypted in bytes, whereas number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4 for Versal For Versal Net, byte aligned data is acceptable
Address to the buffer of GCM tag size, where the API updates GCM tag

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_UNALIGNED_SIZE_ERROR - Size is not aligned for non-last chunk
Update the data to AES engine

briefdescription.para: This function sets AES engine to update key and IV during decryption of secure header or footer of encrypted partition.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
XSECURE_AES_ENABLE_KUP_IV_UPDATE to enable KUP and IV update, XSECURE_AES_DISABLE_KUP_IV_UPDATE to disable KUP and IV update

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred
Enable/disable the KUP and IV update as per the configuration provided

briefdescription.para: This function gives the AES next block length after decryption of PDI block.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Pointer to a 32 bit variable where next block length will be updated

listitem.para: XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

briefdescription.para: This function zeroizes the selected AES key storage register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Select the key source which needs to be zeroized

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - When key zeroization is success
XST_INVALID_PARAM - if key source is invalid
XSECURE_AES_STATE_MISMATCH_ERROR - If State mismatch is occurred

memberdef.argsstring: (const XSecure_Aes *AesInstance, const u32 *KeyPtr, const u32 *DataPtr, u32 *OutputPtr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesDpaCmDecryptData

briefdescription.para: This function updates data and key to the AES core in split mode with DPACM enabled.

parameterdescription.para: InstancePtr Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Key Pointer
Data Pointer
Output where the decrypted data to be stored

listitem.para: XSECURE_AESDPACM_KAT_WRITE_KEY_FAILED_ERROR - Error when AESDPACM key write fails
XSECURE_AESDPACM_KAT_KEYLOAD_FAILED_ERROR - Error when AESDPACM key load fails
XSECURE_AESDPACM_SSS_CFG_FAILED_ERROR - Error when AESDPACM sss configuration fails
XSECURE_AESDPACM_KAT_FAILED_ERROR - Error when AESDPACM KAT fails

simplesect.para: This function is used during DPACM KAT where key and data are updated in split mode with DPACM enabled

listitem.para: Configure AES engine in split mode to update data and key to aes core
Enable PMC DMA Src channel for byte swapping.
Enable PMC DMA Dst channel for byte swapping.
Configure the PMC DMA Tx/Rx for the incoming Block.
Disable PMC DMA Dst channel for byte swapping.
Copy the initial status of DPACM

briefdescription.para: This function waits for AES engine to complete key loading.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - If the AES engine completes key loading
XST_FAILURE - If a timeout has occurred

briefdescription.para: This function enables or disables the GMAC configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
User choice to enable/disable GMAC

simplesect.para: To generate GMAC on AAD data, this API must be called with IsGmacEn set to TRUE just before the last AAD update. After the last AAD update XSecure_AesEncryptFinal or XSecure_AesDecryptFinal must be called to generate or validate the GMAC tag.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_Aes *InstancePtr, u64 AadAddr, u32 AadSize, u64 GcmTagAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_AesUpdateAadAndValidate

briefdescription.para: This function is used to update the AES engine with Additional Authenticated Data(AAD) and validate.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XSecure_Aes instance
Address of the additional authenticated data
Size of additional authenticated data in bytes, whereas number of bytes provided should be multiples of 4
GCM tag of the additional authenticated data

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS AAD updation and validation is success
XSECURE_AES_GCM_TAG_MISMATCH User provided GCM tag does not match calculated tag
Push the AAD to AES engine
Push the GCM TAG to AES engine

briefdescription.para: This file contains AES hardware interface APIs.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
4.0 vns 04/24/2019 Initial release 4.1 vns 08/06/2019 Added AES encryption APIs har 08/21/2019 Fixed MISRA C violations vns 08/23/2019 Initialized status variables 4.2 har 01/03/2020 Added checks for return value of XSecure_SssAes vns 02/10/2020 Added DPA CM enable/disable function rpo 02/27/2020 Removed function prototype and static keyword of XSecure_AesKeyLoad, XSecure_AesWaitForDone functions har 03/01/2020 Added code to soft reset once key decryption is done rpo 03/23/2020 Replaced timeouts with WaitForEvent and code clean up rpo 04/02/2020 Added Crypto KAT APIs Added support of release and set reset for AES bvi 04/07/2020 Renamed csudma as pmcdma vns 04/12/2020 Reset Versal key clear register in AES initialize call 4.3 ana 06/04/2020 Updated NextBlkLen in Xsecure_Aes structure wherever required Updated Aes state kpt 06/29/2020 Added asserts for input arguments and minor enhancements on AES state kpt 07/03/2020 Added type casting for the arguments in XPmcDma_64BitTransfer kpt 07/08/2020 Removed dummy code and Status value reinitialized to XST_FAILURE har 07/12/2020 Removed magic number from XSecure_AesKeyZero har 07/21/2020 Corrected input parameters for config in XSecure_AesCfgKupKeynIv kpt 08/06/2020 Replaced magic numbers with macro's kpt 08/18/2020 Added volatile keyword to status variable in case of status reset td 08/19/2020 Fixed MISRA C violations Rule 10.3 rpo 09/10/2020 Asserts are not compiled by default for secure libraries rpo 09/21/2020 New error code added for crypto state mismatch am 09/24/2020 Resolved MISRA C violations har 09/30/2020 Added blind-write checks for XSecure_AesCfgKupKeynIv Deprecated Family Key support Replaced repetitive code for DMA configuration with XSecure_AesPmcDmaCfgByteSwap function har 10/12/2020 Addressed security review comments am 10/10/2020 Resolved Coverity warnings 4.5 am 11/24/2020 Resolved MISRA C and Coverity warnings har 02/12/2021 Separated input validation checks for Instance pointer har 03/02/2021 Added support for AES AAD kpt 03/21/2021 Added volatile keyword for SStatus variable in XSecure_AesDecryptKat to avoid compiler optimization. am 05/21/2021 Resolved MISRA C violations 4.6 har 07/14/2021 Fixed doxygen warnings kpt 07/15/2021 Added 64bit support for XSecure_AesWriteKey kal 08/19/2021 Renamed XSecure_AesPmcDmaCfgByteSwap to XSecure_AesPmcDmaCfgAndXfer har 08/23/2021 Updated AAD size check kpt 09/18/2021 Added redundancy in XSecure_AesSetDpaCm 4.7 har 01/03/2022 Updated Status and StatusTmp as volatile in XSecure_AesWriteKey() har 01/20/2022 Added glitch checks for clearing keys in XSecure_AesWriteKey() har 02/16/2022 Updated Status with ClearStatus only in case of success 5.0 kpt 07/24/2022 Moved XSecure_AesDecryptKat into XSecure_Kat.c and fixed bug in XSecure_AesDecryptCmKat kpt 08/02/2022 Zeroized user key in XSecure_AesDecryptCmKat kpt 08/19/2022 Added GMAC support dc 08/26/2022 Optimized the code 5.1 kal 09/27/2022 Pass AesDmCfg structure as reference instead of value to XSecure_AesPmcDmaCfgAndXfer function skg 10/13/2022 Added Encrypt/Decrypt update error handling check 5.2 yog 07/10/2023 Added support of unaligned data sizes for Versal Net kpt 07/09/2023 Added AES ECB mode support for versalnet ng 07/13/2023 Added SDT support kpt 07/20/2023 Added volatile keyword for SStatus variable in XSecure_AesDecryptFinal kpt 07/20/2023 Renamed XSecure_AesDpaCmDecryptKat to XSecure_AesDpaCmDecryptData kpt 07/27/2023 Initialize KeySizeInWords to zero to avoid invalid value in case of glitch vss 09/11/2023 Fixed Coverity warning EXPRESSION_WITH_MAGIC_NUMBERS and MISRA-C Rule 10.1 violation vss 09/11/2023 Fixed MISRA-C Rule 8.13 violation vss 09/11/2023 Fixed MISRA-C Rule 10.3 and 10.4 violation 5.3 kpt 11/28/2023 Add support to clear AES PUF,RED,KUP keys har 03/20/2024 Add support for non-word aligned data in XSecure_AesEncryptData and XSecure_AesDecryptData kpt 03/22/2024 Fix overrun issue 5.4 yog 04/29/2024 Fixed doxygen warnings. vss 10/23/2024 Removed AES duplicate code vss 10/28/2024 Removed END label in XSecure_AesDecryptUpdate vss 11/20/2024 Fix for data corruption of GCM tag when any other operation uses DMA0 after encrypt update. vss 01/22/2025 Added status check in AesUpdate and CopyGcmTag functions. 5.6 aa 07/15/2025 Typecast to essential datatypes to avoid implicit conversions and added explicit parenthesis for sub-expression mb 09/10/2025 Exclude cache validation for RISC-V processor. 5.6 rpu 08/11/2025 Added crypto check in XSecure_AesOpInit. mb 10/06/2025 Optimize Status variable usage. tus 10/10/2025 Fix ECB mode state transition to XSECURE_AES_INITIALIZED 5.7 tbk 04/07/2026 Optimized key zeroization by directly writing combined key clear mask to registers mb 04/17/2026 Update crypto check for AES/SHA/RSA
